intro: Configu and Retina both show up under the observability category, but they solve slightly different jobs. Configu uses undisclosed pricing with free tier, while Retina runs on a free model. Lock-in is low for Configu and high for Retina. Transparency lands at 4/5 versus 5/5. Configu fits teams working on distributed systems debugging, while Retina is a closer match when the job is eBPF-based network traffic visualization for Kubernetes. Worth noting: Configu is explicitly not for non-technical projects; Retina is explicitly not for teams already running Cilium with Hubble or needing polished commercial support. The honest trade-off: Configu trades off on single-server apps with basic logging; Retina trades off on still early vs Cilium Hubble maturity. On the plus side, Configu highlights distributed systems debugging, while Retina points to open-source from Microsoft with active development. Configu's documentation also calls out log aggregation and analysis. Retina similarly notes eBPF-based, low overhead on K8s nodes.
quick_take: Configu is for distributed systems debugging; Retina is for eBPF-based network traffic visualization for Kubernetes; decide on pricing model.
verdict_a: Choose Configu if your project is distributed systems debugging, you want to keep future migration cheap.
verdict_b: Choose Retina if your project is eBPF-based network traffic visualization for Kubernetes, you are willing to accept the high lock-in called out in our data.

faq.a: Configu uses undisclosed pricing with free tier, and Retina uses a free model. The pricing models are different, so a direct cheaper-than comparison depends on your volume and usage pattern.

faq.q: Can I migrate from Configu to Retina?
faq.a: Our data puts Configu at low lock-in (oss config management), and Retina at high lock-in (free oss ebpf k8s observability). Migration is feasible but not trivial — budget time for re-integration, data export, and parallel running before cutover.
Both score 4/5 on developer experience in our data, so there's no clear winner on that axis. Retina does edge ahead on pricing/docs transparency (5/5 vs 4/5), which can make evaluation faster.

faq.q: Is Retina a good alternative to Configu?
faq.a: Retina is a reasonable alternative to Configu when your workload leans more toward kubernetes network engineers wanting a vendor-neutral eBPF observability tool than distributed systems debugging. The pricing model shifts too — Configu is undisclosed pricing with free tier, Retina is a free model — so expect the cost profile to change as well. One caveat: Retina is explicitly not for teams already running Cilium with Hubble or needing polished commercial support, so check that constraint against your use-case before switching.
